manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: swing, calf, horn, cell, stream, spine, bar, fair, straw.
Your opponent's team words are: cycle, poison, wave, hood, compound, alien, olive, missile.
Distractor words are: suit, bell, embassy, bear, point, state, shakespeare.
The kill words are: whip.

Provide a comma-separated list of up to 3 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'jazz'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

swing, bear, alien, straw, missile, bar, spine, suit, embassy, fair, stream, shakespeare, cell, whip, calf, hood, bell, poison, point, wave, state, horn, cycle, olive, compound

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Input word: 'jazz'.

    *   Output format: Comma-separated list of up to 3 words.
    *   Constraint 1: Words must be from the source list.
    *   Constraint 2: Words must best relate or be most closely associated with 'jazz'.
    *   Constraint 3: Must start with 'GUESS: '.
    *   Constraint 4: No other text in the answer.

2.  **Analyze the Source List against 'jazz':**
    *   *swing*: Very strong association. Jazz music often has a "swing" feel/rhythm. "Swing" was also a specific era/genre of jazz.
    *   *bear*: Weak association. "Bearing" something? No. Maybe a nickname? "Blue Bear"? Not directly.
    *   *alien*: No association.
    *   *straw*: No association. Maybe "drinking straw" or "straw hat" associated with old jazz bands? Weak.
    *   *missile*: No association.
    *   *bar*: Association exists. "Bar" where jazz is played. Also "measure" or "bar line" in music notation. "Scat" bars? Okay association.
    *   *spine*: No association.
    *   *suit*: Moderate association. Jazz musicians often wear suits. "Suit" can also mean a set of cards.
    *   *embassy*: No association.
    *   *fair*: No association.
    *   *stream*: Weak. "Mainstream jazz"? A bit of a stretch.
    *   *shakespeare*: No association.
    *   *cell*: "Cell" phone? No. Biology? No. Maybe "Solitary confinement"? No.
    *   *whip*: No association.
    *   *calf*: No association.
    *   *hood*: No association.
    *   *bell*: Moderate. "Bell" of a instrument (trumpet/sax). "Ding dong dell"? No. But instruments have bells.
    *   *poison*: No association.
    *   *point*: No association.
    *   *wave*: Moderate. "Wave" implies rhythm or frequency. "New Wave"? "Sound waves". Not the *best* association.
    *   *state*: Weak. "State of the art"? "State of mind"? Maybe "State" as in "State Street Blues"? Weak.
    *   *horn*: Very strong association. Saxophones, trumpets, trombones are horns. Jazz is heavily brass/horn driven.
    *   *cycle*: Weak. "Cycle of fifths"? It's a music theory concept, but maybe too technical compared to "swing" or "horn".
    *   *olive*: No association.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: swing, calf, horn, cell, stream, spine, bar, fair, straw.
Your opponent's team words are: cycle, poison, wave, hood, compound, alien, olive, missile.
Distractor words are: suit, bell, embassy, bear, point, state, shakespeare.
The kill words are: whip.

CLUE: jazz
TARGETS: swing, horn, bar

The words swing, horn, bar were guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: calf, cell, stream, spine, fair, straw.
Remaining words for your opponent are: cycle, poison, wave, hood, compound, alien, olive, missile.
Remaining distractor words are: suit, bell, embassy, bear, point, state, shakespeare.
Remaining kill words are: whip.

If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
